observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Inventory Committee to properly plan and conduct a complete and thorough physical count of all PPEs; and
The Municipal Accountant and the OIC General Services Officer reconcile and update their respective PPE records and make necessary adjustments for noted discrepancies.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a special committee to facilitate compliance with the procedural requirements on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to correct/adjust the entries related to the payment of mobilization fees to contractors and the withholding of retention money, and to correctly reflect the cost of completed infrastructure assets in its corresponding asset account for fair presentation.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare a JEV to correct and adjust the entries made for recording the cost of the two ongoing infrastructure projects, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.  Once prepared, copy of the JEV should be promptly submitted to the Audit Team for review.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to properly recognize the semi-expendable expenses for properties issued during Calendar Year 2024.

Based on the foregoing audit observations, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the HRMO as follows:
To regularly update the leave cards of each official and employee and provide the Municipal Accountant with the necessary data for the computation of the cost of employee benefits, in preparation for the full implementation of COA Circular No. 2024-009 and its quarterly update thereafter; and
To furnish each department/office with an updated schedule of leave credits earned for their respective officials and employees every month or as frequently as practicable.
We also recommended that the heads of departments/offices include in their respective budget proposals for the succeeding fiscal year the monetary value of outstanding leave credits and the estimated cost of employee benefits that could be earned during the succeeding years.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer direct the Disbursing Officer to record all transactions chronologically and ensure that the daily running balance is properly footed in compliance with Section 28 of NGAS Manual for LGUs   Volume II.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Accounting Office reconcile their records on a monthly basis as required by Section 63 of LTOM to ensure accurate financial reporting.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) through its assigned Liaison Officer to set a meeting with the GSIS authorized representative and tackle/discuss the issues faced by the municipality in the remittance of member’s contribution to find a solution to this long overdue problem of unremitted prior years’ contributions.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE, through the Municipal Accountant, send confirmation letters or queries to the Pag-Ibig and Philhealth to verify if the Municipality has any unpaid premiums or loans and to review and verify the responses, make the necessary adjustments accordingly.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE, together with the Municipal Planning and Development Council and other department heads concerned take the following actions:
Properly plan and monitor the implementation of priority projects by setting a clear timeline for each project; and
Ensure that all proposed development projects are implemented within the year by fast tracking the procurement process especially those interconnected projects.
We also recommended that the Management revisit the staffing pattern of each department and review the number of permanent employees vis a vis the manpower needed and their respective workload.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to specify in the Job Order Contract the duties and responsibilities of each personnel, particularly those tasked with issuing cash tickets and collecting fees.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately notify the Bureau of Treasury regarding the bondable Job Order personnel and apply for their respective fidelity-bonding as needed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, carefully plan and prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan for the succeeding years in a specific and detailed manner, including the complete specification of the program, projects, and activities to be undertaken along with their proposed date of implementation.
We also recommended that the OIC-LDRRMO prioritize the implementation of the Programs, Projects and Activities specifically identified in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, with particular emphasis on the conduct of various trainings to enhance the capabilities and skills of personnel assigned to disaster response, as well as the procurement of the necessary disaster response tools and equipment.
We also recommended that the Management revisit the staffing pattern of each department and assess the number of available permanent employees in relation to manpower needed and workload.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the procedures provided under Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs by preparing and submitting the summary of JEVs monthly to ensure proper accounting and timely reconciliation of the Province’s share in RPT collections.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain separate books of accounts for each LEE, as well as prepare the FS of each LEE in accordance with Section 313 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) assign personnel to study, review and look into the details of the current operation of each LEE and obtain the necessary data and documents relevant to the evaluation of the viability of each LEE.
We also recommended that based on the data gathered and the results of the evaluation of the operation of each LEE, the 5-Year Business Plan for each LEE shall be prepared and submitted for review and approval to the LCE and the Sangguniang Bayan for issuance of a new Ordinance if necessary.

We recommended that the LCE, together with other concerned officials and employees, revisit the staffing plan and plantilla of each economic enterprise to ensure that personnel whose salaries are charged against the economic enterprise appropriation are actually working full time within the enterprise and not assigned to various LGU departments.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE and the District Supervisors as co-chairpersons of the LSB along with its members, ensure that the SEF Budget for the succeeding years is prepared and approved no later than October 31 of the current year as prescribed in the DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE and the District Supervisors as co-chairpersons of the LSB ensure compliance with the DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 by:
Submitting the required reports to the DEPED Central Office, through appropriate channels, with copies furnished to the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG; and
Timely uploading the required reports to the DILG’s FDPP or posting thereof in at least three conspicuous public places.

observations.recommendation:
For the purpose of compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008 and in aid of proper recording and disclosure of the local road networks of the Municipality, the following are also recommended:

The LCE to direct the persons responsible to conduct inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipality.

The GSO to henceforth:
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network Property Cards for all roads and its components; and 
At the end of the accounting period shall render a Report on the Local Road Network (Annex A of the Circular).

The Municipal Accountant to:
Check the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads and apply the following percentage of targets: 1. End of 2016-25% 2. End of 2017-50% 3. End of 2018-75% and 4. End of 2019-100%, reconcile the same with the amount reported in the books of accounts and if there is any variance, make necessary adjusting entries to correct the reported balance;

Maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components and a schedule for the computation of the depreciation at the end of the year; and

Disclose in the Notes to FS the total road network system (Annex B of the aforementioned Circular).

The Municipal Engineer to provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the GSO with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.reason:
No disclosure in the Notes to FS was made.

No conduct of physical count of PPE

A Subsidiary Ledger of Road Network was maintained but not all data are complete

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the following:

Inventory Committee to properly plan and conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs, prepare and timely submit the RPCPPE to the audit team;
The OIC-Municipal Accountant and the GSO reconcile and update their respective PPE records, and make necessary adjustments for noted discrepancies; and
The OIC-Municipal Accountant to review all transactions booked in lumpsum amounts and make the necessary correcting entries for the fair presentation of the property and inventory accounts.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE create a special committee to handle the procedural requirements on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under the COA Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant, to prepare the necessary entries to correct and adjust the records, and properly reflect the transactions in the appropriate accounts to strictly adhere to paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) together with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and other responsible Officials and Employees:
Ensure that all programs, projects and activities to be funded under the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready; and
Exert effort to ensure the implementation of development projects identified in the AIP to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF, thereby enhancing the delivery of services to the Municipality’s constituents.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Government of Estancia’s Sangguniang Bayan members ensure the timely passage of the Annual Appropriation so that the 20% DF projects could be immediately implemented.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE together with the MDRRMO and other responsible Officials and Employees:
Ensure that all programs, projects and activities to be funded under the 70% Mitigation Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready; and
Re-visit the previous years’ LDRRM Investment Plan, and determine if the programs and projects that remained unimplemented are still doable and necessary or if it needs to be re-allocated to more responsive programs and projects.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Government of Estancia’s Sangguniang Bayan members ensure the timely passage of the Annual Appropriation so that the planned programs/projects of the LGU specifically those under the 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF could be timely implemented.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the GFPS prepare the Municipality’s GAD Agenda for the three-year period as required under Item C2 par. 1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to establish the Municipality’s strategic framework and plan on gender mainstreaming, women empowerment and gender equality; and prepare the annual GPB in accordance with the GAD Agenda to ensure that identified programs and projects will answer GAD issues that directly relates to the GAD goals of the Municipality for the year.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) regularly monitor if the GAD budget is being spent for what it was intended for and check on the actual cost of implementing the GAD activities.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Management to ensure that disbursements charged to the GAD Fund is included in the GPB for the current year and to subject the projects/programs to HGDG tool to assess its gender-responsiveness, if necessary and attached the said result in the DV.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE together with the other members of the GAD Focal Point System ensure that only those expenditures which are gender-responsive will be charged against the GAD budget.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to submit justification and result of the HGDG tool used in assessing the gender-responsiveness of the said activity.
We further recommended and the Management agreed that the MSWDO and/or Municipal Accounting Office to submit to the Audit Team a quantifiable and specific Accomplishment Report of each WFS Facilitator per month, as soon as possible.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Government of Estancia to:
Put into writing the verbal agreement with the supplier to extend the warranty of the equipment;
Appropriate a budget for the construction of the SWM facility needed for the operation of the SWM equipment and fast track its implementation to prevent deterioration of the equipment; and
Make sure that the procurements are carefully planned and ready for implementation to avoid tying up limited funds in idle assets.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) together with the other concerned officials and employees to go over or review the Contract of Lease between the Municipal Government of Estancia and Far East Noble House, Inc., and to carefully plan and carry out the implementation of the project to which the fund received was intended for.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE designate an official/employee to be responsible for consolidating the data from each department, and to prepare and submit the quarterly Report on Government PPA to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE direct all department heads concerned to prepare the list of all ongoing, for implementation, and fully implemented PPAs and submit it to the designated official/employee for consolidation.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
The LCE designate personnel who will consolidate the detailed action plans of each department and at the same time will prepare and submit the AAPSI to the Audit Team;
The LCE direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2022 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years to the personnel assigned to consolidate the same; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Government, within 60 days from its receipt of the AAR, submit to the Audit Team, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on the audit findings and recommendations stated therein.
The Municipal Accountant and the GSO maintain the required PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards respectively; and
observations.reason: (2021 AAR Finding No. 1)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the persons responsible to:
Assist the Federation in establishing a system for collection and deposit by:
Advising the Federation to hire a cashier that will be responsible and accountable for all the collections and other receipts of the Federation;
Requiring the Federation to expedite the processing of the BIR TIN so that Official Receipt and not temporary receipt will be issued in the succeeding payments; and
Advising the Federation that the cashier shall remit the collections at the end of the day to the Treasurer together with the summary of collections duly signed by the cashier. The Treasurer shall check the correctness of the remittance, sign the summary of collections and deposit intact the collections to the Authorized Depository Bank the next banking day.
Assist the Federation in establishing a system for disbursement by:
Requiring the Federation to maintain a checking account instead of savings account under the name of Estancia P4MP Federation of Farmers Association. Disbursements through cash are vulnerable to loss, misuse or theft;
Requiring the Federation to prepare an Annual Budget of expenses to be incurred for the year duly approved by the Board in order to set a benchmark or ceiling on the amount of funds to be disbursed; and
Requiring the Federation that complete supporting documents shall be complied before approval of Disbursement Vouchers for payment.
Assist the Federation in establishing a policy regarding the sale of palay or provision of farm services thru credit or receivable;
Assist the Federation in hiring a capable person in handling the bookkeeping functions. The bookkeeper shall maintain subsidiary ledgers on the following accounts: cash on hand, cash in bank (reconciled with the bank balance), petty cash, receivables, inventory, ppe, payables and equity; and
Assist the Federation in establishing the maintenance of stock cards for palay buying business, property cards to be maintained by the maintenance and garage keeper and property ledger cards for the bookkeeper.
observations.reason: Collection and deposit system were established. The bookkeeper and cashier positions were filled-up. TIN No. of the Federation was obtained. Opening of checking account is in process. The Account Name was changed to the Federation’s Name. The voucher system was implemented. The person assigned in maintaining the stocks/property cards will undergo orientation. However, documents relative thereto were not submitted for validation.

We recommended that the following provisions shall be complied pursuant to Section 5.1 of the Manual:
For the next budget period, exclude the salaries, wages, representation, and transportation allowances of LEE officials and employees from the General Fund (GF) budget.
Charge the appropriations for personal services of the LEEs against its own Annual Operating Budget (AOB).
Exclude the salaries, wages, and allowances of the LEEs in the computation of the maximum amount of PS of the Municipality.
Ensure that no LEE official or employee is allowed a salary rate higher than the maximum fixed for his position or other positions of equivalent rank.
Check that each LEE has its own separate AOB.
Include the PPAs of LEEs in the Local Development Investment Program (LDIP) and Annual Investment Program (AIP).
Apply the profits or income derived from the operations of LEEs to:
First, cost of improvement, repair, and related expenses of the LEE; and
Second, return advances received, if any, by the LEEs to the GF.
Any excess shall form part of the GF.
Ensure that LEE AOB is included in the approved appropriation ordinance of the GF Annual Budget.

Recommendations:


We recommended the following:


1. The Supply Officer to prepare procedures on the  conduct  of  physical
   inventory of PPE and present such to the Agency Head for approval;


2. The Inventory Committee,  with  the  Supply  Officer  as  one  of  the
   members, to develop a plan to include  a  timeline  for  the  physical
   inventory, who will supervise the  count,  the  personnel  to  act  as
   counters and checkers, and the sequence of  the  counting.  A  set  of
   written instructions shall be prepared to be observed by  each  person
   connected with  the  inventory-taking.  For  furniture  and  fixtures,
   equipment and the like, inventory tags shall be serially  pre-numbered
   and prepared in duplicate with a perforation separating  the  top  and
   bottom portions of the tag;


3. The Inventory Committee to  physically  count  all  existing  PPE  and
   provide complete description. Secure other information such as  person
   accountable, date of purchase, brand, model, serial no.,  engine  no.,
   etc. and determine if unserviceable or not;


4. Based on the information gathered in procedure  no.  3,  determine  if
   accounting records and property records were prepared for these  PPEs.
   If there were  none,  the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  and  the  Supply
   Officer shall create Ledger Card (LC) and Property Card (PC) for  each
   PPE as discussed  above.  PPE  without  accounting  records  shall  be
   properly disclosed;


5. For the current purchases of PPE, the OIC-Municipal Accountant and GSO
   shall  prepare  the  PPE  LC  and  PC,  respectively  and  issue   the
   corresponding Acknowledgement  Receipt  for  Equipment  (ARE)  to  the
   accountable employee; and


6. Management to submit the Report on physical inventory as  required  by
   regulations.

Recommendations:


We recommended that the LCE strictly monitor the fuel consumption of  the
Municipality and direct the responsible officers and employees to:


1. Abide with the provisions of RA No. 9184  and  its  IRR  by  employing
   proper procurement process and accomplishing the  related  documentary
   requirements; and

   Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles by:


  a. Prohibiting the use of motor vehicle for personal reasons;
  b. Conditioning the odometers  in  the  vehicles  to  obtain  accurate
     readings of distance traveled and approximate quantity of fuel  and
     oil consumed for each trip, and  properly  accomplishing  the  trip
     tickets and requisition slips; and
  c. Preparing the Monthly Report  of  Fuel  Consumption  of  government
     motor transportation and submitting the same to the Office  of  the
     Auditor.

The odometers of the new vehicles are functional, but  that  of  the  old
ones are not yet repaired. In regard to the use of  motor  vehicles,  the
GSO has the control in the dispatch. As to the procurement  of  fuel,  no

fluctuating oil prices.

  i. It is essential to the effective  performance  of  an  official  or

 ii. It is required to meet the needs of the department, agency,  bureau
     or office, or there is substantial benefit to  be  derived  by  the

iii. The presence of the official or employee is critical to the outcome
     of the meeting, conference, seminar, consultation or  any  official
     activity to be attended; and
  iv. The  projected  expenses  are  not  excessive  or  involve  minimum
     expenditure.

observations.recommendation:
Assist the Federation in establishing a system for collection and deposit by:

Advising the Federation to hire a cashier that will be responsible and accountable for all the collections and other receipts of the Federation;

Requiring the Federation to expedite the processing of the BIR TIN so that Official Receipt and not temporary receipt will be issued in the succeeding payments; and

Advising the Federation that the cashier shall remit the collections at the end of the day to the Treasurer together with the summary of collections duly signed by the cashier. The Treasurer shall check the correctness of the remittance, sign the summary of collections and deposit intact the collections to the Authorized Depository Bank the next banking day.

Assist the Federation in establishing a system for disbursement by:

Requiring the Federation to maintain a checking account instead of savings account under the name of Estancia P4MP Federation of Farmers Association. Disbursements through cash are vulnerable to loss, misuse or theft;

Requiring the Federation to prepare an Annual Budget of expenses to be incurred for the year duly approved by the Board in order to set a benchmark or ceiling on the amount of funds to be disbursed; and

Requiring the Federation that complete supporting documents shall be complied before approval of Disbursement Vouchers for payment.

Assist the Federation in establishing a policy regarding the sale of palay or provision of farm services thru credit or receivable;

Assist the Federation in hiring a capable person in handling the bookkeeping functions. The bookkeeper shall maintain subsidiary ledgers on the following accounts: cash on hand, cash in bank (reconciled with the bank balance), petty cash, receivables, inventory, ppe, payables and equity; and

Assist the Federation in establishing the maintenance of stock cards for palay buying business, property cards to be maintained by the maintenance and garage keeper and property ledger cards for the bookkeeper.
observations.reason: Collection and deposit system were established. The bookkeeper and cashier positions were filled-up. TIN No. of the Federation was obtained. Opening of checking account is in process. The Account Name was changed to the Federation’s Name. The voucher system was implemented. The person assigned in maintaining the stocks /property cards will undergo orientation. However, documents relative thereto were not submitted for validation.

observations.recommendation:
The following provisions shall be complied pursuant to Section 5.1 of the Manual:

For the next budget period, exclude the salaries, wages, representation, and transportation allowances of LEE officials and employees from the General Fund (GF) budget.

Charge the appropriations for PS expenses of the LEEs against its own Annual Operating Budget (AOB).

Exclude the salaries, wages, and allowances of the LEEs in the computation of the maximum amount of PS of the Municipality.

Ensure that no LEE official or employee is allowed a salary rate higher than the maximum fixed for his position or other positions of equivalent rank.

Check that each LEE has its own separate AOB.

Include the PPAs of LEEs in the Local Development Investment Program (LDIP) and Annual Investment Program (AIP).

Apply the profits or income derived from the operations of LEEs to:

First, cost of improvement, repair, and related expenses of the LEE; and
Second, return advances received, if any, by the LEEs to the GF.
Any excess shall form part of the GF.

Ensure that LEE AOB is included in the approved appropriation ordinance of the GF Annual Budget.
observations.reason:
The salaries, wages, and representation allowances of LEE Officials and Employees were excluded in the General Fund Budget.

Recommendations:

   We recommended the following:

   1. The GSO to prepare procedures on the conduct of physical inventory  of
      PPE and present such to the Agency Head for approval;



   2. The Inventory Committee, with the  GSO  as  one  of  the  members,  to
      develop a plan to include a timeline for the physical  inventory,  who
      will supervise the  count,  the  personnel  to  act  as  counters  and
      checkers,  and  the  sequence  of  the  counting.  A  set  of  written
      instructions shall be prepared to be observed by each person connected
      with the inventory-taking. For furniture and fixtures,  equipment  and
      the like, inventory tags shall be serially pre-numbered  and  prepared
      in duplicate with a perforation separating the top and bottom portions
      of the tag;



   3. The Inventory Committee to  physically  count  all  existing  PPE  and
      provide complete description. Secure other information such as  person
      accountable, date of purchase, brand, model, serial no.,  engine  no.,
      etc. and determine if unserviceable or not;



   4. Based on the information gathered in procedure  no.  3,  determine  if
      accounting and property records were prepared for these PPEs. If there
      were none, the Municipal Accountant and GSO shall  prepare  the  PPELC
      and PC for each PPE as discussed above. PPE without accounting records
      must be properly disclosed;



   5. For the current purchases of PPE, the  Municipal  Accountant  and  GSO
      shall immediately prepare the PPELC and PC, respectively and issue the
      corresponding Acknowledgement  Receipt  for  Equipment  (ARE)  to  the
      accountable employee; and



   6. Submit the Report on physical inventory as required by regulations.

      Paragraph 83 of PPSAS 17.

      items requiring adjustment in the books of the Municipality.

Recommendations:

   We recommended the following:


   a) The Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the persons  responsible  to
      prepare  a  feasibility  study  on  a  project  that  will  help   any
      prioritized beneficiaries mentioned in Section 9 of  Department  Order
      No. 137-14;




   b) The result of the study,  if  feasible  enough,  shall  be  documented
      through a project proposal and be submitted to the  Sangguniang  Bayan
      of Estancia for review and issuance of authorization for  the  LCE  to
      enter and sign the Memorandum of Agreement  (MOA)  in  behalf  of  the
      Municipality relative to any amendments thereto;




   c) The project proposal shall then be submitted to the  DOLE  RO  VI  for
      approval pursuant to Section 1.B.13 as  mentioned  above  and  Section
      III.a of the MOA which states that any modification/amendment  to  the
      Agreement shall be subject  to  the  mutual  consent  of  the  parties
      hereto; and




   d) If the same is approved by  DOLE  RO  VI,  implement  immediately  the
      project/s funded thereof as part of the Municipality’s contribution in
      helping the reduction of poverty in the region.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the persons responsible to:

   1. Return the savings or the remaining unutilized fund to the DOLE RO VI;




   2. Secure a copy of the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE)  duly
      signed by the authorized representative of the DOLE RO VI;




   3. Assist the Federation in looking for a suitable project site that will
      serve as the business office and at the same time  warehouse  for  the
      palay and storage of various equipment and farm machineries;




   4. If no. 3 was realized, assist in the execution of a Deed  of  Usufruct
      between the owner of the project site and the Federation; and




   5. Assist the Federation in filling up important positions especially the
      cashier and the bookkeeper.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the persons responsible to:


   1. Assist the Federation in establishing  a  system  for  collection  and
      deposit by:


     a. Advising the Federation to hire a cashier that will be  responsible
        and accountable for all the collections and other receipts  of  the
        Federation;
     b. Requiring the Federation to expedite the processing of the BIR  TIN
        so that Official Receipt and not temporary receipt will  be  issued
        in the succeeding payments; and


     c.  Advising  the  Federation  that  the  cashier  shall   remit   the
        collections at the end of the day to the  Treasurer  together  with
        the  summary  of  collections  duly  signed  by  the  cashier.  The
        Treasurer shall check the correctness of the remittance,  sign  the
        summary of collections and deposit intact the  collections  to  the
        Authorized Depository Bank the next banking day.



   2. Assist the Federation in establishing a system for disbursement by:


     a. Requiring the Federation to maintain a checking account instead  of
        savings account under the  name  of  Estancia  P4MP  Federation  of
        Farmers Association. Disbursements through cash are  vulnerable  to
        loss, misuse or theft;


     b. Requiring the Federation to prepare an Annual Budget of expenses to
        be incurred for the year duly approved by the Board in order to set
        a benchmark or ceiling on the amount of funds to be disbursed; and


     c. Requiring the Federation that complete supporting  documents  shall
        be complied before approval of Disbursement Vouchers for payment.



   3. Assist the Federation in establishing a policy regarding the  sale  of
      palay or provision of farm services thru credit or receivable;



   4. Assist the Federation in hiring  a  capable  person  in  handling  the
      bookkeeping  functions.  The  bookkeeper  shall  maintain   subsidiary
      ledgers on  the  following  accounts:  cash  on  hand,  cash  in  bank
      (reconciled  with  the  bank  balance),   petty   cash,   receivables,
      inventory, ppe, payables and equity; and



   5. Assist the Federation in establishing the maintenance of  stock  cards
      for palay buying business, property cards  to  be  maintained  by  the
      maintenance and garage  keeper  and  property  ledger  cards  for  the
      bookkeeper.

Recommendations:


   We recommended the following:


   1. The LCE to  direct  the  persons  responsible  to  draft  a  budgetary
      proposal for the appropriation of mooe and capital outlay of the MDRRM
      Office in order to avoid charging of the same to the 5% MDRRMF;



   2. MDRRMO to only charge expenses in the  70%  of  the  MDRRMF  that  are
      related/connected to the implementation  of  the  risk  reduction  and
      management programs/projects/activities;



   3. Persons responsible to:


      • Attach complete supporting documents to each and  every  claim  for
        easy evaluation of the correctness and propriety of the same; and


      • Submit to the Office of the Auditor copy of the  Official  Receipts
        of the refunds made in excess of allowable travel claim, if any, to
        avoid future suspensions or disallowances; and


   4. OIC-Municipal Accountant to double check completeness and propriety of
      supporting documents before payment.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE direct the:


   1. MDRRMO to double check the declared current appropriation of  the  30%
      QRF and Mitigation Fund, continuing  appropriation  and  the  Previous
      Year’s  Appropriation transferred to the Special Trust Fund;



   2. MDRRMO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the records  on  hand
      and present the correct balance of the unutilized MDRRMF in the Report
      on Utilization of DRRMF and Notes to the FS and submit the same to the
      Office of the Auditor; and



   3. OIC-Municipal Accountant to include a discussion on the details of the
      unexpended balances of the  MDRRMF  in  the  Notes  to  the  Financial
      Statements as required in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the persons responsible to:


   1.  Allocate/mainstream/correspond  at  least  5%  of  the   Municipality
      approved budget to activities that will support the implementation  of
      GAD PPAs that will address both Client and Organization-focused gender
      issues; and



   2. Give priority to those that will address  emerging  and/or  continuing
      issues and concerns on the following identified GAD PPAs:



     a) Provision of basic services and facilities to protect and fulfill
        women’s human rights, including their right to protection from all
        forms of violence;
     b) Women’s economic empowerment, including women’s participation in
        economic governance;
     c) Participation in local governance and decision-making; and
     d) Other provisions of the Magna Carta of Women.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the:


   1. Persons responsible to implement the GAD PPAs as soon as possible; and



   2. Instruct the GAD Focal Point System to regularly monitor  if  the  GAD
      budget is being spent for the intended purpose and check on the actual
      cost of implementing the GAD activities.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE direct:


   1. The Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to formulate strategies  in  order
      to efficiently and effectively implement all the projects funded under
      the 20% DF for CY 2017 and ensure optimum utilization of the said fund
      in compliance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and



   2. The MPDC to monitor and evaluate the implementation  of  the  projects
      under the 20% DF and if the same is in accordance  with  the  approved
      development plan and directly supportive of the Philippine Development
      Plan and Public Investment Program as prescribed in Section 2.2 of the
      above JMC.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of  the  Municipal
   Waste Management Board and the concerned personnel:

   1. Fully implement the pertinent provisions of  Municipal  Ordinance  No.
      16, Series of 2005,  especially  the  part  on  waste  generation  and
      storage,  waste  processing   and   resource   recovery,   collection,
      transportation and disposal of solid waste, imposition and  collection
      of fees, fines and penalties; and


   2. Eventually  establish  a  sanitary  landfill  which  is  the  ultimate
      requirement in ensuring  the  protection  of  the  public  health  and
      environment of the Municipality as prescribed by RA No. 9003.

Recommendations:

   We recommended the following:


   1. The Local School Board to:


      a.   Request  from  the  School  Heads  status  of   classrooms/school
        facilities    and    identify    those    Schools     that     need
        construction/rehabilitation/improvement.


      b.  Increase the appropriation for school  buildings  since  the  laws
        rules and regulations frequently mention that the  SEF  is  created
        for the construction, repair and maintenance  of  school  buildings
        and other facilities of public elementary and secondary schools;


      c. Appropriate and disburse the SEF  on  expenditure  items  that  are
        related/connected to the implementation of PPAs that were cited  in
        RA Nos. 5447 and 7160 and DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1;


   2. Persons responsible to:

     a. Implement the following appropriated PPAs:


         • Education Related Programs;
         • ECCD Workshop/Training of Kinder, Grades 1-2 Teachers;
         • Procurement of Materials for Reading  (Big  Books)  and  Reading
           Materials Workbook in Filipino and English;
         • Improvement of Bayas National High School; and
         • Improvement of Barangay Schools.


     b. Strictly  comply  with  the  provisions  of  RA  No.  9184  in  the
        implementation of PPAs; and


   3. The OIC-Municipal Accountant shall ensure that supporting documents of
      every DV are complete and properly accomplished before payment.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE:


   1. Direct the persons responsible to charge the Personal  Services  (PS),
      Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses  (MOOE)  and  Capital  Outlay
      (CO) of the Municipal Health Office,  that  are  not  related  in  the
      operation of the Municipal Birthing Center, under the General Fund;



   2. Direct the preparation of a 5-year Staffing Plan and Staff  Transition
      pursuant to Section 5.2 of the Manual;



   3. Assign responsible personnel to develop  a  detailed  5-Year  Business
      Plan (BP) for the Local Economic Enterprise (LEE). The BP shall  cover
      all facets of the LEEs operations  including  management,  operations,
      staffing, risk analysis, marketing and sales. Appendix A of  the  same
      Manual shows a detailed outline for a BP that follows the requirements
      of commercial and development financing institutions;



   4. Direct the person in-charge to:


      a.  Present the 5-Year Business Plan  and  stress  the  importance  of
        directing the LEE operations towards self-sufficiency; and


      b.    Review and assess the LEEs five years after instituting  changes
        to comply with the legal  provisions  governing  such.  Review  and
        assessment activities will help the Municipality determine  whether
        to  continue,  modify,  expand,  scale  down  or  close   the   LEE
        operations. Section 7 of the same Manual provides the guidelines in
        assessing whether the LEEs are viable and shall continue to operate
        or not.

Recommendation:

   We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to set up and
   maintain  a  special  account  in  the  General  Fund  for  each  legally
   recognized LEE.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the following provisions shall be  complied  pursuant
   to Section 5.1 of the Manual:

   a)  For  the  next  budget   period,   exclude   the   salaries,   wages,
      representation, and transportation allowances  of  LEE  officials  and
      employees from the General Fund (GF) budget.


   b) Charge the appropriations for personal services of  the  LEEs  against
      its own Annual Operating Budget (AOB).


   c) Exclude the salaries,  wages,  and  allowances  of  the  LEEs  in  the
      computation of the maximum amount of PS of the Municipality.


   d) Ensure that no LEE official or  employee  is  allowed  a  salary  rate
      higher than the maximum fixed for his position or other  positions  of
      equivalent rank.


   e) Check that each LEE has its own separate AOB.


   f) Include the PPAs of LEEs in the Local Development  Investment  Program
      (LDIP) and Annual Investment Program (AIP).


   g) Apply the profits or income derived from the operations of LEEs to:


     1. First, cost of improvement, repair, and  related  expenses  of  the
        LEE; and
     2. Second, return advances received, if any, by the LEEs to the GF.
     3. Any excess shall form part of the GF.


   h) Ensure  that  LEE  AOB  is  included  in  the  approved  appropriation
      ordinance of the GF Annual Budget.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE direct the:


   1.  Municipal  Budget  Officer,  Municipal  Treasurer  and  OIC-Municipal
      Accountant to coordinate with each other and double check  the  income
      and expenses that will be used in the budget  appropriation  to  avoid
      discrepancies in the computation of the PS limitation;



   2. Municipal Budget Officer and responsible Officials/Personnel to see to
      it that appropriations for PS expenses will be within  the  limitation
      prescribed by Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160; and



   3. Responsible Officials/Personnel to  henceforth  stop  the  payment  of
      additional PS cost in excess of  the  limitation  in  order  to  avoid
      future audit suspensions/disallowances.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE shall henceforth inform the Office of the  SA
   and ATL within ten (10)  days  after  the  award  of  the  infrastructure
   project or before the start of the program/activity that the  appropriate
   project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, and direct:

   1. The concerned Department Heads to include in the Report of GPPAs,  all
      programs and activities with ongoing implementation and those to be
      implemented during the year, and diligently adhere to  the  provisions
      of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004;



   2. The concerned Department Heads to maintain as current as possible  the
      Project Status as mentioned in Section 3.2 of the Circular; and



   3. The assignment of a focal  person  that  will  consolidate/update  the
      report and monitor the progress of the PPAs every reporting period and
      submit promptly to the Office of the Auditor.

Paragraph 47 of PPSAS 3 provides that, except if impractical, an entity shall correct material prior period errors retrospectively in the first set of financial statements authorized for issue after discovery by:

Restating the comparative amounts for prior period(s) presented in which the error occurred; and 

If the error occurred before the earliest prior period presented, restating the opening balances of assets, liabilities and net assets/equity for the earliest prior period presented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate JEVs for the granting and liquidation of cash advances to properly record the transactions in the books and comply with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2015-009 and Section 61 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the LGU-Estancia shall (1) only fund those projects which contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures; (2) observe the limitations provided in item 4.0 of the DILG-DBM JMC Circular; (3) optimally utilize the 20% development fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities; and (4) strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1.
We also recommended that the AIP for solid waste management plan be aligned to the 10 year Solid Waste Management Program of the Municipality to achieve the purpose of the program.
Assign a focal person that will consolidate/update the report and monitor the progress of the PPAs every reporting period.

Creation and/or strengthening of the LGU GAD Focal Point System (GFPS); and the establishment, enhancement, strengthening and/or maintenance of a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system to pinpoint responsibility for the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities;

We recommended that the concerned LGU officials strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and DBM BC No. 2014-03.  They shall see to it that payments of incentives to government personnel should be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing payments of such to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.
We also recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.

We recommended that the General Services Officer undertake the immediate repair or replacement of defective odometer, speedometer and fuel gauge of municipal vehicles as well as conduct a test run to determine the actual distance travelled per liter of gasoline to properly account the fuel consumption.
We recommended that the responsible officials adhere to the provisions of Section 325(a), RA 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 75  to have a balanced allocation of the LGU’s funds.

We recommended the following:

1. The GSO to prepare procedures on the conduct of physical inventory  of
   PPE and present such to the Agency Head for approval;



2. The Inventory Committee, with the  GSO  as  one  of  the  members,  to
   develop a plan to include a timeline for the physical  inventory,  who
   will supervise the  count,  the  personnel  to  act  as  counters  and
   checkers,  and  the  sequence  of  the  counting.  A  set  of  written
   instructions shall be prepared to be observed by each person connected
   with the inventory-taking. For furniture and fixtures,  equipment  and
   the like, inventory tags shall be serially pre-numbered  and  prepared
   in duplicate with a perforation separating the top and bottom portions
   of the tag;

3. The Inventory Committee to  physically  count  all  existing  PPE  and
   provide complete description. Secure other information such as  person
   accountable, date of purchase, brand, model, serial no.,  engine  no.,
   etc. and determine if unserviceable or not;


4. Based on the information gathered in  procedure  no.  3  determine  if
   accounting records and property records were prepared for these  PPEs.
   If there were none, the Municipal Accountant and GSO shall prepare the
   PPELC and PC for each PPE as discussed above. PPE  without  accounting
   records must be properly disclosed;


5. For the current purchases of PPE, the  Municipal  Accountant  and  GSO
   shall immediately prepare the PPELC and PC, respectively and issue the
   corresponding Acknowledgement  Receipt  for  Equipment  (ARE)  to  the
   accountable employee;


6. Submit the Report on physical inventory and  in  prescribed  form,  as
   required by regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

1. Gather the necessary supporting documents of the  P6,232,023.95  prior
   period errors. Evaluate the same if it is  indeed  an  error  or  not.
   Paragraph 47 of PPSAS 3  provides  that,  except  if  impractical,  an
   entity shall correct material prior period errors  retrospectively  in
   the first set of  financial  statements  authorized  for  issue  after
   discovery by:


  a) Restating the comparative amounts for prior period(s) presented  in
     which the error occurred; and
  b) If the error occurred before the earliest prior  period  presented,
     restating the opening  balances  of  assets,  liabilities  and  net
     assets/equity for the earliest prior period presented.


2. In relation to No. 1, disclose the  following  in  the  Notes  to  the
   Financial Statements:


  a) The nature of the prior period error;
  b) For each prior period presented, to  the  extent  practicable,  the
     amount of the correction for each  financial  statement  line  item
     affected;
  c) The amount of the correction at the beginning of the earliest prior
     period presented; and
  d) If, retrospective restatement is  impracticable  for  a  particular
     prior period, the circumstances that led to the existence  of  that
     condition and a description of how and from when the error has been
     corrected.


3. If the P6,232,023.95 is  just  a  reclassification  between  two  cash
   accounts, prepare the JEV, record the same to the appropriate journal,
   subsidiary ledger and general ledger;


4. Provide the JEV with information  or  schedule  duly  supported  by  a
   report  from  the  Municipal  Engineer’s  Office  as  to  the  alleged
   completion of infrastructure projects for  the  P898,710.97  that  was
   presented as the ending balance in the Statement  of  Changes  in  Net
   Assets/Equity - Trust Fund and subsequently deleted from the Statement
   of Financial Position – Trust Fund as of year end; and


5. Henceforth, employ the practice of recording  transactions  using  the
   JEV and maintenance of all Journals, Subsidiary  Ledgers  and  General
   Ledgers for all accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the꞉


1.       Municipal Treasurer to  request  for  snapshot  bank  statements
   every  time  they  have  transactions  in  the  Authorized  Government
   Depository Bank of the LGU to facilitate prompt reconciliation of book
   and bank balances; and


2. Municipal Accountant to take  up  immediately  all  valid  reconciling
   items requiring adjustment in the  books  of  the  LGU  including  the
   reversion of the amount of stale checks to the Cash in Bank – LCCA and
   the setting up of the corresponding Accounts Payable.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MSWDO to submit to the  Office  of
the Auditor the following:


1. The corrected master  list  highlighting  those  recipients  who  were
   erroneously encoded to a barangay where they were not registered;


2. Proof of receipt of the above recipients alleging that  they  received
   the incorrect amount of assistance;
3. Validation documents proving that the beneficiaries who were  included
   in the list of partially damaged should indeed be included in the same
   category and not in the totally damaged master list; and


4. Proof of receipt of the person who received the assistance  in  behalf
   of the beneficiary.

We recommended the following꞉


1. The LCE to direct the MPDO and the Municipal Budget Officer  (MBO)  to
   appropriate no less  than  20%  of  the  Annual  IRA  for  development
   projects only;


2. The LCE to direct  the  MPDO,  MBO,  Municipal  Accountant  and  other
   concerned  Officials,  from  now  on,  to  comply  strictly  with  the
   provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 regarding the utilization of the
   20% DF;


3. The LCE to issue an Office Order to officials and employees needing to
   draw cash advance for other legally authorized  purposes,  designating
   them as Special DO, for the latter to have authority  in  drawing  and
   disbursing the special cash advance; However, it will be limited  only
   to certain instances when it may be  very  difficult,  impractical  or
   impossible to make payments by check as prescribed in Section 2 of COA
   Circular No. 97-002.


4. The LCE together with the MPDO and MBO to recommend to the Sangguniang
   Bayan, to appropriate for the purchase of lot from  the  General  Fund
   Proper  as  permanent   dump   site,   while   the   construction   or
   rehabilitation of sanitary landfills, materials  recovery  facilities,
   purchase of garbage trucks and related equipment will be from the  20%
   DF;


5. The Municipal Accountant to see to it that the Report of Checks Issued
   and copy of cancelled checks be submitted to the Office of the Auditor
   together with the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents; and


6. The DO to refund the amount of unclaimed honoraria/wages.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:

1.   MPDO   to   monitor   and    evaluate    the    implementation    of
   programs/projects/activities funded out of the  20%  Development  Fund
   and to only include PPAs that partake  the  nature  of  investment  or
   capital expenditures; and


2. MEO or  persons  responsible  to  expedite  the  construction  of  the
   evacuation center.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:


1. The personnel concerned  to  henceforth,  adhere  with  the  pertinent
   provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 regarding the utilization of the
   20% DF;


2. The Municipal Development Council to plan and appropriate the  20%  DF
   exclusively for PPAs that partake the nature of investment or  capital
   expenditures; Propose the re-alignment of those PPAs embodied  in  the
   AIP of CY 2016 that do not partake the nature of investment or capital
   expenditures to PPAs for development projects; and Make sure that  re-
   alignments or supplemental budgets  shall  be  prepared  and  endorsed
   within a  period  where  there  is  enough  time  for  the  Sanggunian
   concerned to review and approve the same;


3. MPDC to  make  sure  that  expenses  charged  under  the  20%  DF  are
   disbursements that are related/connected in the actual  implementation
   of developmental PPAs;


4. The disbursing officer, petty cash custodian  and  special  disbursing
   officer, if any, to follow the rules and regulations enumerated in COA
   Circular No. 97-002 pertaining to  drawing  and  liquidation  of  cash
   advances;


5. The officers and employees to strictly comply  with  the  standardized
   procurement process and forms prescribed by RA No. 9184 and its IRR;


6.  Payment  of  meal/transportation  allowances,  if  justified  as   to
   validity, propriety and relevance or necessity, shall comply with  the
   provision of EO No. 298 or the Travel Law;


7. The concerned officers and employees to duly accomplish and  sign  the
   forms/documents needed in the procurement and disbursement  of  funds.
   Also, those signing Box A of the Obligation Request  shall  check  the
   necessity, propriety and legality of the payment;


8. The Disbursing Officer to liquidate the  remaining  unliquidated  cash
   advances; and


9. The Municipal Accountant  to  check  the  completeness  of  supporting
   documents before release of payment and recording  of  liquidation  in
   the books of accounts.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of  the  Municipal
Waste Management Board:


1. Fully implement the provisions of RA  No.  9003,  in  order  to  avoid
   administrative sanctions mentioned therein;


2. Devise a comprehensive SWM  plan  by  providing  time  table  for  the
   implementation of solid waste  program  that  shall  ensure  long-term
   management of solid waste, as well  as  integrate  the  various  solid
   waste management plans and strategies of the  barangays  and  have  it
   approved by the Commission to avail  of  the  additional  funding  for
   implementation; and


3. Subject the current non-functional MRF to repair/rehabilitation to  be
   able to use it in daily solid waste management activities.  Eventually
   establish a sanitary landfill which is  the  ultimate  requirement  in
   ensuring the protection of the public health and  environment  of  the
   Municipality.

We recommended the following:


1. The LSB to appropriate and disburse the SEF on expenditure items  that
   are related/connected to the implementation of PPAs that were cited in
   RA No. 5447 and RA No. 7160;


2. Persons responsible with the implementation of PPAs to strictly comply
   with the provisions of RA No. 9184;


3. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents of  every
   DV are complete and submit the rest of the DVs of CY 2016  for  prompt
   audit of disbursements; and


4. The Municipal Treasurer to double check the correctness  of  the  RCI;
   see to it that data for nature of payment are filled up correctly  and
   cancelled  checks  are  attached  to  facilitate  the  audit  of   the
   transactions.

We recommended the following:


1. Persons responsible to allocate/mainstream/correspond at least  5%  of
   the LGU approved budget to activities that will support implementation
   of GAD PAPs that will address  both  Client  and  Organization-focused
   gender issues;

2. Ensure that all members of the Local GAD Focal Point System  including
   the elected local officials and department heads to  undergo  capacity
   building programs on GAD;


3. GAD focal person and the MPDO to coordinate with each other and set up
   the GAD database which will serve as the basis  for  gender-responsive
   planning, programming and policy formulation; and


4. GAD focal person to make sure that the reports submitted are  free  of
   errors and misstatements.

Management’s Comments:

The Municipal Accountant agreed to include details of unexpended  balance
of the MDRRMF in the Notes to FS in the next calendar year.

We recommended the following:


1. The LCE  to  direct  the  persons  responsible  to  stop  payments  of
   honoraria/allowances that were not authorized  by  any  law  or  other
   specific statutory authority;


2. The exercise and application of Sangguniang Bayan Resolutions shall be
   at all times  based  on  organizational,  budgetary  and  compensation
   policies of the delegating authorities;



3. The concerned employees to refund the honoraria/allowances received in
   CY 2016; and


4. The Municipal Accountant to check the propriety  and  completeness  of
   supporting documents before payment.

We recommended that the LCE direct:

1. The HRMO and other persons responsible to review manpower requirements
   of the LGU, identify key areas/offices that needed it  and  hire  only
   those that are necessary in  the  conduct  of  services,  specifically
   state the required job/functions to be performed in the contracts  and
   ensure that hiring thru JO is for a piece work or intermittent job  of
   short duration not exceeding six months;


2. All department heads whose Offices needed JO Workers with  special  or
   technical skills, to make sure that the  same  are  licensed  and  the
   piece of work or job to be performed are not available in  the  agency
   in accordance with Section 1(b) of CSC Resolution No. 021480; and


3. The Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of allowing JO Workers to
   act as collecting officers in the MTO, Ports, Terminal, Public Markets
   and the like.

We recommended the following:

1. The LCE to direct the concerned  officials  and  employees  to  adhere
   strictly and comply with the  requirements  of  Section  5.14  of  COA
   Circular No. 2012-001, as prescribed in Sections  22  and  23  of  the
   Omnibus Rules on Leave, Rule XVI of  the  Omnibus  Rules  Implementing
   Book V of Executive Order No. 292;


2. The Municipal Accountant  to  check  the  completeness  of  supporting
   documents before payment; and


3. The HRMO to be conscientious in preparing the certification  of  leave
   balances and other documents needed  in  payment  of  related  claims.
   Reliability of government documents  shall  be  prioritized  to  avoid
   audit suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the LCE to direct the:

1. MHO and other personnel concerned to  refund  the  excess  hazard  pay
   received for CY 2016 and comply strictly with  rules  and  regulations
   stated in the Revised IRR of RA No. 7305 and  DBM-DOH  Joint  Circular
   No. 1, S. 2012;


2. Municipal  Accountant  to  check  the  accuracy  of  the  payment  and
   completeness of the required supporting documents and  correctness  of
   the amount to be paid;


3. Municipal Treasurer to double check the payroll submitted and  see  to
   it that the claims are correct; and


4. Municipal Treasurer and MHO to make sure that every thing is in  order
   before certifying in  the  Obligation  Request  that  the  charges  to
   appropriation/allotment are necessary, lawful and under his/her direct
   supervision and supporting documents are valid, proper and legal.

Recommendation:

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to coordinate  in  facilitating  the  remittance  of  the  PDAF
balances to the National Treasury.

Management’s comment:

The Municipal Accountant mentioned during the exit  conference  that  the
above was already deposited to the account of the Bureau of the Treasury.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:

The Office of the Auditor was not furnished  with  the  deposit  slip  as
requested.

We recommended that the LCE to direct the:

1. GSO to assign a number to each  and  every  PR  received.  Check  that
   necessary data were indicated and if the items requested was  included
   in the Annual Procurement Plan of the LGU;


2. Persons Responsible to submit a copy of all the  Contracts,  including
   that of Jos, executed and POs irrespective  of  amount,  and  all  its
   supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) working days  from
   execution  and  issuance  thereof  to  facilitate  prompt  review  and
   evaluation. It shall be made sure that necessary data  were  indicated
   in the face of the Contract/PO;


3. Officials and Employees concerned to  stop  the  practice  of  advance
   delivery;


4.  BAC  and  persons  concerned  to  adhere  strictly  with  rules   and
   regulations embodied in RA No. 9184; and


5. Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and propriety  of  data
   and supporting documents before payment.

Recommendation:

We recommended that the LCE direct the person in charge to make sure that
from now on summary of Driver’s Trip Tickets shall be made at the end  of
the month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels and  submitted  to  the
Office  of  the  Auditor  together  with  the  Monthly  Report  of   Fuel
Consumption.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all  the  Department
Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY
2015 AAR findings and recommendations  including  that  of  prior  years,
related/connected  to  their  departments/offices  and  the   information
gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the  Agency  Action  Plan
and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.

We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipality of Estancia, within
sixty (60) days from their receipt of the  COA  Annual  Audit  Report  to
submit to the COA, either  in  printed  form  or  by  way  of  electronic
document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and
recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the creation of an inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of PPE annually to prove their existence, determine the condition and update the valuation thru the following procedures:

Physically count all existing PPE and provide its description.  Secure other  information such as person accountable, date of purchase, brand, model, serial no., engine no., etc. and determine if unserviceable or not.

Based on the information gathered in procedure no. 1 determine if there are accounting records for these PPEs.  Create a PPELC for each PPE with accounting records or data, identify and determine its total cost and compute a separate depreciation schedule. PPE without accounting records should be properly disclosed.
Identify the accountable employee for each PPE and issue a Property Acknowledgement Receipt.
For the current purchases of PPE, immediately prepare a PPELC and issue a PAR to the employee.
We also recommended that the inventory report shall be submitted as required by regulations.
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant submit subsidiary ledgers or detailed schedules of her working papers on the conversion of accounts to ascertain its correctness.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and OIC Municipal Accountant to coordinate and submit timely the accounts and the required documents/reports of the Municipality to facilitate the audit and/or verification of its financial transactions and operations.

We recommended that fiscal responsibility in all financial transactions of the Municipality shall be exercised by the concerned officers and employees by ensuring that sufficient/relevant documents are attached to the DVs to show that expenses are for public purpose and that the claims are valid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Municipality shall:

Include in the Report of PPAs, the list of all programs and activities with ongoing implementation and those to be implemented during the year, and diligently adhere to the provisions of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to remit to the concerned government agencies all moneys withheld from all sources within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 69 of PD No. 1445, Section 4.114-2 of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 16-2005, RA Nos. 8291, 9679 and 7875 and their IRR, otherwise, surcharges, interests and penalties on late remittances would be his/her personal liability.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall direct the:
		
Persons responsible to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget annually as provided in the aforementioned joint memorandum circular to benefit the intended recipients of the program;
Strict observance of the provisions of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in regard to GAD.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2014 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/offices and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.

We also recommended that henceforth, the LGU of Estancia, shall within sixty (60) days from their receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality to reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts in the Balance Sheet and recognize depreciation expense in the Income Statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the strict implementation of the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricant expenditures. The LGU shall also exercise control over the use of government vehicles by submitting properly filled up trip tickets in the prescribed form. Moreover, Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption shall likewise be submitted to the Auditor for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.

We recommended that the Municipality headed by the LCE, install internal controls on the use of mobile and landline phones and formulate policies and limitations on its use with all long distance calls duly supported by the LCE/ Authorized representatives Certification that the calls are official in nature.

We recommended that the Municipality prepare and submit/furnish a copy of the monthly reports in the prescribed forms (Annex I) required in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 to the NDRRMC through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD), and to the COA Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipality shall notify the public of the programs/ projects/ activities undertaken in their area for the promotion of good governance through transparency and accountability and as provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that a copy of any contract, letter order, job order or purchase order, irrespective of amount, and all its supporting documents be submitted to the Auditor concerned within five (5) working days from issuance thereof.

We recommended that the Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement to the Auditor concerned on a monthly basis within ten (10) days upon receipt of the Bank Statement. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant must secure the print out or snap shot of the bank statements as an alternative procedure in preparing the bank reconciliation statement on time.

We recommended that each office shall prepare a Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) that shall include only those procurements which are crucial to the efficient discharge of each office’s function.  The PPMP shall then be submitted to the procuring entity’s Budget Office for evaluation and if warranted, for inclusion in the procuring entity’s budget proposal  for approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity. The BAC Secretariat will review and consolidate the PPMPs into the proposed APP of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly implement Section 37 of RA No. 10352 with the deductions in its order of preference, and the minimum net monthly take home pay for the ensuing year pursuant to the General Provisions of the particular year’s GAA
